questions.question:
Consider the following function written in the C programming language.

   void foo(char *a){
     if ( *a && *a != '  '){
          foo(a+1);
          putchar(*a);
       }
}
The output of the above function on input \("ABCD \; \; EFGH"\) is

(GATE CSE 2015 SET-2)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
The output of the following C program is__________.

  void f1(int a, int b) {
    int c;
    c=a; a=b; b=c;
}
void f2(int *a, int *b) {
    int c;
    c=*a; *a=*b; *b=c;
}
int main(){
    int a=4, b=5, c=6;
    f1(a,b);
    f2(&b, &c);
     printf("%d",c-a-b);
}


(GATE CSE 2015 SET-1)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the C function given below

 int f(int j)
{
static int i = 50;
int k;
if (i == j)
{
printf("something");
k = f(i);
return 0;
}
else return 0;
}
Which one of the following is TRUE?

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-2)
options:
A.

The function returns 0 for all values of j.

B.

The function prints the string something for all values of j.

D.

The function will exhaust the runtime stack or run into an infinite loop when j = 50
questions.correct_answer:
D.

The function will exhaust the runtime stack or run into an infinite loop when j = 50
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following function

 double f (double x) {
if ( abs (x*x - 3) < 0. 01) return x;
else return f (x / 2 + 1.5/x);
}

questions.question:
Consider the function func shown below:

 int func(int num) {
int count = 0;
while (num) {
    count++;
    num >> = 1;
}
return (count);
}
The value returned by func(435)is __________.

(GATE CSE 2014 SET-2)

questions.question:
What is the return value of f(p,p), if the value of p is initialized to 5 before the call? Note that the first parameter is passed by reference, whereas the second parameter is passed by value.

 int f (int &x, int c) {
  c = c - 1;
  if (c==0) return 1;
   x = x + 1;
return f(x,c) * x;
}


(GATE CSE 2013)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following C code segment.


int a, b, c = 0;
void prtFun(void);
main( )
{ static int a = 1; /* Line 1 */
   prtFun( );
   a += 1;
   prtFun( );
   printf(" \n %d %d ", a, b);
}
void prtFun(void)
{ static int a = 2; /* Line 2 */
   int b = 1;
   a += ++b;
   printf(" \n %d %d ", a, b);
}
What output will be generated by the given code segment if:

Line 1 is replaced by auto int a = 1;
Line 2 is replaced by register int a = 2;

(GATE CSE 2012)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following recursive C function that takes two arguments

 unsigned int foo(unsigned int n, unsigned int r) {
  if (n  > 0) return (n%r +  foo (n/r, r ));
  else return 0;
}
What is the return value of the function foo when it is called as foo (513, 2)?

(GATE CSE 2011)

questions.question:
What is the value printed by the following C program?

 #include < stdio.h >
int f(int * a, int n)
{
    if(n<=0)return 0;
    else if(*a% 2 ==0) return *a+f(a+1,n-1);
    else return *a-f(a+1,n-1);
}
int main ( )
{
    int a[ ] {12, 7, 13, 4, 11, 6};
    printf ("%d", f(a,6));
    return 0;
}


(GATE CSE 2010)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the program below:

 # include < stdio.h >
int fun(int n, int * f_p) {
   int t, f;
   if (n <=1) {
         *f_p =1;
          return 1;
   }
   t = fun (n-1, f_p);
   f = t+*f_p;
   *f_p = t;
   return f;
}
int main() {
    int x = 15;
    printf ("%d\ n", fun(5,& x));
    return 0;
}
The value printed is

(GATE CSE 2009)

questions.question:
Consider the code fragment written in C below :

void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n <= 1)  {
        printf ("%d", n);
    }
    else {
        f (n/2);
        printf ("%d", n%2);
    }
}
Which of the following implementations will produce the same output for f(173) as the above code?
P1
void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n/2)  {
        f(n/2);
    }
    printf ("%d", n%2);
}

P2
 void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n <=1)  {
        printf ("%d", n);
    }
    else {
        printf ("%d", n%2);
        f (n/2);
    }
}


(GATE IT 2008)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
